description: Robert M. Cover — For James Wm. Moore: Some Reflections on a Reading of the Rules (84 Yale L.J. 718 (1975))
show_notes:
Robert M. Cover — For James Wm. Moore: Some Reflections on a Reading of the Rules (84 Yale L.J. 718 (1975))

Robert Cover's tribute to James Wm. Moore reflects on what it means to read the Federal Rules of Civil Procedure. He argues that procedure is never merely neutral or trans-substantive: the Rules embody contestable political and ideological commitments about the relationship between process and substance. A meditation on the jurisprudence of procedure and the values secreted in its interstices.
